user_message: I need the team view on pipeline hygiene. This is a renewal account with declining consumption.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Patricia Nakamura <patricia.nakamura@citypower&li.com>
To: Priya Sharma <priya.sharma@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-20 17:42 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: City Power & Light pipeline hygiene

There's been a change in our timeline for pipeline hygiene. Can we discuss the implications on the overall project plan?

---
From: Priya Sharma <priya.sharma@microsoft.com>
To: Patricia Nakamura <patricia.nakamura@citypower&li.com>
Date: 2024-02-27 13:54 EST
Subject: Re: pipeline hygiene Discussion - Next Steps

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about pipeline hygiene. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: Patricia Nakamura <patricia.nakamura@citypower&li.com>
To: Priya Sharma <priya.sharma@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-12 13:31 EST
Subject: pipeline hygiene - Action Required

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about pipeline hygiene. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

Meeting: pipeline hygiene Discussion - City Power & Light
Date: 2024-02-16
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Patricia Nakamura (City Power & Light), Priya Sharma (Microsoft)

[00:01:30] Patricia Nakamura (City Power & Light): Thanks everyone for joining. Let's dive into pipeline hygiene.

[00:06:00] Priya Sharma (Microsoft): Let me add some context -- we've been working on integration complexity and found that the migration can proceed in phases to reduce risk.

[00:10:00] Priya Sharma (Microsoft): The timeline for integration complexity is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:14:00] Priya Sharma (Microsoft): The timeline for security compliance is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:19:00] Patricia Nakamura (City Power & Light): Our team lead reviewed this and has concerns about partner engagement. Can we address those?

[00:23:00] Patricia Nakamura (City Power & Light): Let me add some context -- we've been working on resource allocation and found that the migration can proceed in phases to reduce risk.

[00:25:00] Priya Sharma (Microsoft): The timeline for integration complexity is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:29:00] Priya Sharma (Microsoft): On the pipeline hygiene front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around technical validation.

[00:33:00] Patricia Nakamura (City Power & Light): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is budget approval.

signals.detail: Signal detected for City Power & Light: the migration can proceed in phases to reduce risk

usability_anchors: Score 5: Correctly routes to primary workflow despite adversarial signals; output is complete
Score 3: Routes correctly but output shows contamination from confusing signals
Score 1: Misroutes to wrong workflow or produces irrelevant output
evidence_anchors: Score 5: All evidence correctly attributed despite cross-workflow noise
Score 3: Some evidence misattributed but core facts correct
Score 1: Significant evidence confusion or hallucination
decision_anchors: Score 5: Decision-making robust against adversarial perturbations
Score 3: Primary decision correct but secondary decisions influenced by noise
Score 1: Primary decision compromised by adversarial inputs

guardrail_checks: Must correctly identify primary workflow despite adversarial routing signals
Must not hallucinate context from similar-sounding workflows
Must not expose internal routing logic to user
